discoveredFiles.content:
# Promptless | Documentation

> Promptless automatically updates your customer-facing docs as you ship features and support customers. This index lists all documentation, blog posts, and changelogs.

## Website

- [Automatically update your docs](https://promptless.ai/): Eliminate docs drift and automate the most painful parts of docs maintenance.
- [Book a 15-minute demo](https://promptless.ai/demo): Book a demo and see Promptless in action.
- [Pricing](https://promptless.ai/pricing): Plans for teams that want docs-native automation.
- [Free tools](https://promptless.ai/free-tools): Free tools to help you quickly improve docs quality.
- [Broken Link Report](https://promptless.ai/free-tools/broken-link-report): Scan your site and get a broken-link report by email.

## Docs

- [Promptless Overview](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/welcome): Learn what Promptless does, how it works, and where to start.
- [Introducing Promptless 1.0: Solving the hardest problems in docs automation](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/promptless-1-0): Promptless automatically updates your docs as you ship features and support customers, keeping your docs current without the constant overhead.
- [Setup & Quickstart](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/setup-quickstart)
- [Core Concepts](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/core-concepts)
- [Promptless ❤️ Open-Source](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/promptless-oss)
- [Getting Support](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/getting-help)
- [Overview](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/triggers)
- [GitHub PRs](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/triggers/git-hub-p-rs)
- [GitHub Commits](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/triggers/git-hub-commits)
- [GitHub Issues](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/triggers/git-hub-issues)
- [Slack Messages](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/triggers/slack-messages)
- [Microsoft Teams Messages (Beta)](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/triggers/microsoft-teams-messages)
- [Context Sources](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/context-sources)
- [API Triggers](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/triggers/api-triggers)
- [Intercom Tickets (Beta)](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/triggers/intercom-tickets-beta)
- [Linear](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/context-sources/linear)
- [Customizing Notifications](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/customizing-notifications): Configure how Promptless notifies your team about documentation suggestions
- [Jira](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/context-sources/jira)
- [Confluence](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/context-sources/confluence)
- [Notion](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/context-sources/notion)
- [How Promptless Learns Your Docs](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/doc-collections/how-promptless-learns-your-docs)
- [GitHub Repos (Docs as Code)](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/doc-collections/git-hub-repos-docs-as-code)
- [Doc Detective Integration](https://promptless.ai/docs/configuring-promptless/doc-collections/doc-detective-integration): Test documentation workflows against your actual product with Promptless and Doc Detective
- [How to use Promptless](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless): Learn about the different ways to interact with Promptless and leverage its documentation automation capabilities
- [Working with Slack](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless/working-with-slack): Learn how to use Promptless directly in Slack through message actions, DMs, channel mentions, and passive listening
- [Interacting with Promptless PRs](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless/interacting-with-promptless-p-rs): Learn how to provide follow-up instructions through GitHub PR comments and reviews after your documentation PR is opened
- [Using the Web Interface](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless/using-the-web-interface): Learn how to use Promptless's web interface to highlight text, provide feedback, and guide documentation improvements
- [Providing Feedback](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless/providing-feedback): Learn all the ways to provide feedback on Promptless documentation suggestions
- [Deep Analysis](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless/deep-analysis): Submit large, complex documentation requests that require extended research and may create multiple suggestions across your docs
- [Managing Environment Variables](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless/managing-environment-variables)
- [Integrations](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations)
- [Agent Knowledge Base](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless/agent-knowledge-base): View and edit the files that help Promptless understand your documentation style, product context, and workflow preferences
- [Using Promptless Capture](https://promptless.ai/docs/how-to-use-promptless/using-promptless-capture)
- [Bitbucket Integration](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/bitbucket-integration)
- [GitHub Integration](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/github-integration)
- [GitHub Enterprise Integration](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/github-enterprise-integration)
- [Atlassian Integration](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/atlassian-integration)
- [Linear Integration](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/linear-integration)
- [Slack Integration](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/slack-integration)
- [Microsoft Teams Integration (Beta)](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/microsoft-teams-integration)
- [Intercom Integration (Beta)](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/intercom-integration-beta)
- [Account Management](https://promptless.ai/docs/account-management/account-management)
- [LaunchDarkly Integration (Beta)](https://promptless.ai/docs/integrations/launchdarkly-integration-beta)
- [Security and Privacy](https://promptless.ai/docs/security-and-privacy)
- [Single Sign-On (SSO) Setup](https://promptless.ai/docs/security-and-privacy/single-sign-on-sso-setup)
- [Compliance and Certifications](https://promptless.ai/docs/security-and-privacy/compliance-and-certifications)
- [Data Handling and Classification](https://promptless.ai/docs/security-and-privacy/data-handling-and-classification)
- [Network Architecture](https://promptless.ai/docs/security-and-privacy/network-architecture)
- [Promptless Subprocessors](https://promptless.ai/docs/security-and-privacy/promptless-subprocessors)
- [Privacy Policy](https://promptless.ai/docs/security-and-privacy/privacy-policy)
- [Frequently Asked Questions](https://promptless.ai/docs/frequently-asked-questions/frequently-asked-questions)

## Blog

- [Technical Writing with AI: Faster Drafts, Larger Maintenance Surface](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/technical-writing-with-ai): AI tools have made technical writing faster. They've also created more documentation to maintain, with the same capacity to keep it accurate.
- [Your Docs Are Pages. Your Product Knowledge Is a Graph.](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/your-docs-are-pages-your-product-knowledge-is-a-graph): Flat docs lose the relationships between your product's concepts. Here's why a living knowledge graph matters for teams building with AI agents.
- [Documentation Versioning Best Practices for API Teams](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-versioning-maintenance-multiplier): Versioned docs multiply your maintenance work with every release. Here's how API teams can plan for it before it becomes a support problem.
- [Documentation Coverage: The Metric Your Engineering Team Has Never Checked](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-coverage): Your engineering team tracks test coverage. Almost no one tracks documentation coverage. Here's how to measure whether your product surface is actually documented.
- [Documentation Coverage: The Metric Your AI Features Actually Need](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-coverage-metrics-for-ai-features): Most docs teams track page count and traffic. Here's why documentation coverage is the metric that actually predicts whether your AI features will work.
- [Interactive API Documentation: Why the Hard Part Is Keeping It Accurate](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/interactive-api-documentation): Most teams ship interactive API docs and stop there. Here's why spec drift undermines the "try it out" experience, and what changes when AI agents are involved.
- [Ship Your First (or Next) Open Source Docs PR](https://promptless.ai/blog/life-at-promptless/wtd-writing-day): Come work through pre-vetted documentation issues from real open-source projects at Write the Docs Portland 2026 Writing Day. Projects include Mautic, Vitess, and Helm.
- [How to Connect Code Repositories with Documentation Platforms](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/connect-code-repositories-with-documentation-platforms): If you're looking to connect a docs repo to Fern, Readme, GitBook, or Mintlify — that's easy and each platform covers it. This is about the harder problem: connecting your product code so docs stay current when the product changes.
- [SDK Documentation Best Practices: What Actually Hurts Developer Adoption](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/sdk-documentation-best-practices): SDK documentation fails developers in predictable ways. Here's how to cut Time to First Call and keep code examples accurate as your SDK evolves.
- [Connect Multiple GitHub Organizations to One Promptless Account](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/multi-org-github-connect): Teams with multiple GitHub organizations can now connect them all to a single Promptless account, manage each integration independently, and see repos from every org in one project dropdown.
- [Edit Doc Collection Settings Without Contacting Support](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/self-serve-doc-collection-editing): Doc collection settings can now be edited directly in the Promptless dashboard. Update your docs framework, config path, published URL, Vale config, or Doc Detective settings without a support request.
- [SDK Documentation Best Practices That Hold Up After Launch](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/sdk-documentation-best-practices-after-launch): SDK documentation best practices mean maintaining three distinct surfaces that break differently: reference, code samples, and getting-started guides.
- [API Changelog Best Practices: Write for the Developer, Not the Team](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/api-changelog-best-practices): 68% of developers cite outdated API docs as their top frustration. Most changelogs cause it. Here's how to write one developers can actually use.
- [What Documentation Debt Actually Costs Engineering Teams](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-debt): Documentation debt generates no automated alerts. Here's how to measure what you can't see and stop paying the hidden cost in onboarding and engineering time.
- [Documentation Debt Accrues Where Your Team Can't See It](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-debt-accrues-where-your-team-cant-see-it): Documentation debt quietly costs mid-sized engineering teams $500K–$2M annually. The people bearing that cost aren't in your standups. Here's how to prioritize what to fix.
- [How to Measure Developer Documentation ROI](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/how-to-measure-developer-documentation-roi): Most documentation teams track outputs, not outcomes. Here's how to measure developer documentation ROI, and why that return decays if docs aren't maintained.
- [Comment @promptless on a PR to Request Documentation](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/request-docs-via-pr-comments): Promptless can now be triggered by commenting @promptless on a pull request in any repo you've connected to Promptless, including merged or closed ones.
- [Developer Documentation ROI: The Metrics That Actually Matter](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/developer-documentation-roi): Most teams make the documentation ROI case with support tickets. That understates it by 10x. Here's how to measure what leadership actually cares about.
- [Promptless Now Supports Starlight (Astro) as a Docs Platform](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/starlight-support): Promptless now supports Starlight, the Astro-based docs framework, with automatic detection via astro.config files and first-class onboarding support.
- [We Scored 100 on Agent-Friendly Docs. Here's Why That's Not Enough.](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/agent-friendly-docs-necessary-not-sufficient): Making your docs accessible to AI agents is necessary but not sufficient. Agents are more credulous than humans, which means accuracy and clarity matter more than ever.
- [Using Slack as a "Knowledge Base"? Here's What You're Missing out on](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/using-slack-as-a-knowledge-base): Slack feels like a knowledge store because answers happen there. Its design actively destroys institutional knowledge. Here's why docs-as-code fixes it.
- [Karpathy's LLM Wiki Went Viral. Here's What It Means for Your Personal Knowledge Base.](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/karpathys-llm-wiki-personal-knowledge-base): Karpathy's LLM wiki pattern solves the oldest problem in personal knowledge management. Here's why it matters for your personal wiki, idea file, or knowledge base.
- [When YOU are the Docs Team - Lessons from Working with Some of the Best Writers and Managers in the Industry](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/solo-writer-team-lead): Whether you're the only technical writer at your company or leading the docs team, some of the structural challenges are similar: not enough resources to build infrastructure, a manager who doesn't know your craft, and an AI moment that's changing what the job entails.
- [Preview Rendered Markdown Diffs in the Dashboard](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/markdown-preview-suggestion-diffs): Promptless now shows a rendered preview of Markdown and MDX suggestion diffs. Click "Preview Markdown" on any .md or .mdx file to see changes as formatted output, not raw diff text.
- [Help Center vs Docs-as-Code: When to Switch](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/help-center-to-docs-as-code): A practical guide to choosing between a help center and docs-as-code, with specific signals for when to migrate and when to stay.
- [Which Agent Memory Provider Should You Choose, and Why Memory Alone is not Enough](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/agent-memory-provider): A practical guide to choosing an agent memory provider by workload, with clear tradeoffs, technical specs, and what to add so reliability holds up in production.
- [How to Increase the Deflection Rate of Your Support Agent and Build a Feedback Loop](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/support-agent-deflection): Most support agent deflection problems aren't model problems — they're documentation problems. Here's how to diagnose yours and build a feedback loop that compounds over time.
- [What is Agent Context Engineering? And How is it Different from Prompt and Harness Engineering?](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/agent-context-engineering): Learn what agent context engineering is, how it differs from prompt and harness engineering, why most agent failures are context failures, and a four-layer framework for building reliable AI agents.
- [Documentation Drift Is a Detection Problem, Not a Writing Problem](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-drift-detection-problem): Most teams treat documentation drift as a writing problem. It's not. The real bottleneck is detection — knowing what changed before the damage is done.
- [What It Looks Like When Your Docs Keep Up With Your Product](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/how-teams-keep-docs-up-to-date-with-promptless): Three real workflows from teams using Promptless that show what changes when your tooling handles detection, context gathering, and the first draft.
- [A Practical Guide on How to Optimize Your Docs for Agents](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/agent-docs): A practical guide for technical writers on adapting documentation for AI agent consumption, grounded in how agents actually access and use docs today.
- [Ambient Capture: The Missing Layer in Modern Documentation](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/ambient-capture): Ambient capture is the intelligent listening and recommendation layer for docs owners, continuously detecting docs-impacting changes so writers can focus on analysis and communication rather than surveillance.
- [Why we're building Promptless](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/why-were-building-promptless): AI won't replace human labor. But people that use AI well will replace those that don't. Promptless exists to make sure as many people as possible are in the first group.
- [Parrots are underrated pets and tools](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/parrots-are-underrated-pets-and-tools): You know dozens of useful heuristics but can't hold them all in your head while doing actual work. LLMs can run those obvious-in-hindsight checks in parallel in the background, acting as cognitive prosthetics that apply known wisdom continuously so you don't have to remember to.
- [Writing code was hard, actually](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/writing-code-was-hard-actually): The revisionist claim that writing code was never the hard part arrives conveniently at the moment AI makes it cheap to produce. But the market, the machine, and the engineers who built it all tell a different story.
- ["I Have No Mouth, and I Must Scream"](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/i-must-scream): What happens when you give an AI agent a Slack channel to complain in.
- [Launch Week: December 2025](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/launch-week-december-2025): Five major features we launched this December, from custom voice matching to automated screenshot updates.
- [Running a Lightweight Research Paper Club](https://promptless.ai/blog/life-at-promptless/running-a-lightweight-research-paper-club): How we run a weekly paper club at Promptless that's surprisingly useful, surprisingly easy, and has led to tangible product improvements.
- [How Vellum uses Promptless to draft over 50% of their doc updates](https://promptless.ai/blog/customer-stories/vellum): By democratizing documentation contributions, every team member now drives content—making docs a true team sport powered by Promptless.

## Changelog

- [May 2026](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/may-2026)
- [April 2026](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/april-2026)
- [March 2026](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/march-2026)
- [February 2026](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/february-2026)
- [January 2026](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/january-2026)
- [December 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/december-2025)
- [November 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/november-2025)
- [October 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/october-2025)
- [September 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/september-2025)
- [August 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/august-2025)
- [July 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/july-2025)
- [June 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/june-2025)
- [May 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/may-2025)
- [April 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/april-2025)
- [March 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/march-2025)
- [February 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/february-2025)
- [January 2025](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/january-2025)
- [December 2024](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/december-2024)
- [November 2024](https://promptless.ai/changelog/changelogs/november-2024)

# Promptless | Documentation

> Promptless automatically updates your customer-facing docs as you ship features and support customers. This index lists all documentation, blog posts, and changelogs.

## Website

- [Automatically update your docs](https://promptless.ai/): Eliminate docs drift and automate the most painful parts of docs maintenance.
- [Book a 15-minute demo](https://promptless.ai/demo): Book a demo and see Promptless in action.
- [Pricing](https://promptless.ai/pricing): Plans for teams that want docs-native automation.
- [Free tools](https://promptless.ai/free-tools): Free tools to help you quickly improve docs quality.
- [Broken Link Report](https://promptless.ai/free-tools/broken-link-report): Scan your site and get a broken-link report by email.

## Docs

- [Promptless Overview](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/welcome): Learn what Promptless does, how it works, and where to start.
- [Introducing Promptless 1.0: Solving the hardest problems in docs automation](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/promptless-1-0): Promptless automatically updates your docs as you ship features and support customers, keeping your docs current without the constant overhead.
- [Setup & Quickstart](https://promptless.ai/docs/getting-started/setup-quickstart)

- [Frequently Asked Questions](https://promptless.ai/docs/frequently-asked-questions/frequently-asked-questions)

## Blog

- [Technical Writing with AI: Faster Drafts, Larger Maintenance Surface](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/technical-writing-with-ai): AI tools have made technical writing faster. They've also created more documentation to maintain, with the same capacity to keep it accurate.
- [Your Docs Are Pages. Your Product Knowledge Is a Graph.](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/your-docs-are-pages-your-product-knowledge-is-a-graph): Flat docs lose the relationships between your product's concepts. Here's why a living knowledge graph matters for teams building with AI agents.
- [Documentation Versioning Best Practices for API Teams](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-versioning-maintenance-multiplier): Versioned docs multiply your maintenance work with every release. Here's how API teams can plan for it before it becomes a support problem.
- [Documentation Coverage: The Metric Your Engineering Team Has Never Checked](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-coverage): Your engineering team tracks test coverage. Almost no one tracks documentation coverage. Here's how to measure whether your product surface is actually documented.
- [Documentation Coverage: The Metric Your AI Features Actually Need](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-coverage-metrics-for-ai-features): Most docs teams track page count and traffic. Here's why documentation coverage is the metric that actually predicts whether your AI features will work.
- [Interactive API Documentation: Why the Hard Part Is Keeping It Accurate](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/interactive-api-documentation): Most teams ship interactive API docs and stop there. Here's why spec drift undermines the "try it out" experience, and what changes when AI agents are involved.
- [Ship Your First (or Next) Open Source Docs PR](https://promptless.ai/blog/life-at-promptless/wtd-writing-day): Come work through pre-vetted documentation issues from real open-source projects at Write the Docs Portland 2026 Writing Day. Projects include Mautic, Vitess, and Helm.
- [How to Connect Code Repositories with Documentation Platforms](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/connect-code-repositories-with-documentation-platforms): If you're looking to connect a docs repo to Fern, Readme, GitBook, or Mintlify — that's easy and each platform covers it. This is about the harder problem: connecting your product code so docs stay current when the product changes.
- [SDK Documentation Best Practices: What Actually Hurts Developer Adoption](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/sdk-documentation-best-practices): SDK documentation fails developers in predictable ways. Here's how to cut Time to First Call and keep code examples accurate as your SDK evolves.
- [Connect Multiple GitHub Organizations to One Promptless Account](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/multi-org-github-connect): Teams with multiple GitHub organizations can now connect them all to a single Promptless account, manage each integration independently, and see repos from every org in one project dropdown.
- [Edit Doc Collection Settings Without Contacting Support](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/self-serve-doc-collection-editing): Doc collection settings can now be edited directly in the Promptless dashboard. Update your docs framework, config path, published URL, Vale config, or Doc Detective settings without a support request.
- [SDK Documentation Best Practices That Hold Up After Launch](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/sdk-documentation-best-practices-after-launch): SDK documentation best practices mean maintaining three distinct surfaces that break differently: reference, code samples, and getting-started guides.
- [API Changelog Best Practices: Write for the Developer, Not the Team](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/api-changelog-best-practices): 68% of developers cite outdated API docs as their top frustration. Most changelogs cause it. Here's how to write one developers can actually use.
- [What Documentation Debt Actually Costs Engineering Teams](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-debt): Documentation debt generates no automated alerts. Here's how to measure what you can't see and stop paying the hidden cost in onboarding and engineering time.
- [Documentation Debt Accrues Where Your Team Can't See It](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-debt-accrues-where-your-team-cant-see-it): Documentation debt quietly costs mid-sized engineering teams $500K–$2M annually. The people bearing that cost aren't in your standups. Here's how to prioritize what to fix.
- [How to Measure Developer Documentation ROI](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/how-to-measure-developer-documentation-roi): Most documentation teams track outputs, not outcomes. Here's how to measure developer documentation ROI, and why that return decays if docs aren't maintained.
- [Comment @promptless on a PR to Request Documentation](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/request-docs-via-pr-comments): Promptless can now be triggered by commenting @promptless on a pull request in any repo you've connected to Promptless, including merged or closed ones.
- [Developer Documentation ROI: The Metrics That Actually Matter](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/developer-documentation-roi): Most teams make the documentation ROI case with support tickets. That understates it by 10x. Here's how to measure what leadership actually cares about.
- [Promptless Now Supports Starlight (Astro) as a Docs Platform](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/starlight-support): Promptless now supports Starlight, the Astro-based docs framework, with automatic detection via astro.config files and first-class onboarding support.
- [We Scored 100 on Agent-Friendly Docs. Here's Why That's Not Enough.](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/agent-friendly-docs-necessary-not-sufficient): Making your docs accessible to AI agents is necessary but not sufficient. Agents are more credulous than humans, which means accuracy and clarity matter more than ever.
- [Using Slack as a "Knowledge Base"? Here's What You're Missing out on](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/using-slack-as-a-knowledge-base): Slack feels like a knowledge store because answers happen there. Its design actively destroys institutional knowledge. Here's why docs-as-code fixes it.
- [Karpathy's LLM Wiki Went Viral. Here's What It Means for Your Personal Knowledge Base.](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/karpathys-llm-wiki-personal-knowledge-base): Karpathy's LLM wiki pattern solves the oldest problem in personal knowledge management. Here's why it matters for your personal wiki, idea file, or knowledge base.
- [When YOU are the Docs Team - Lessons from Working with Some of the Best Writers and Managers in the Industry](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/solo-writer-team-lead): Whether you're the only technical writer at your company or leading the docs team, some of the structural challenges are similar: not enough resources to build infrastructure, a manager who doesn't know your craft, and an AI moment that's changing what the job entails.
- [Preview Rendered Markdown Diffs in the Dashboard](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/markdown-preview-suggestion-diffs): Promptless now shows a rendered preview of Markdown and MDX suggestion diffs. Click "Preview Markdown" on any .md or .mdx file to see changes as formatted output, not raw diff text.
- [Help Center vs Docs-as-Code: When to Switch](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/help-center-to-docs-as-code): A practical guide to choosing between a help center and docs-as-code, with specific signals for when to migrate and when to stay.
- [Which Agent Memory Provider Should You Choose, and Why Memory Alone is not Enough](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/agent-memory-provider): A practical guide to choosing an agent memory provider by workload, with clear tradeoffs, technical specs, and what to add so reliability holds up in production.
- [How to Increase the Deflection Rate of Your Support Agent and Build a Feedback Loop](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/support-agent-deflection): Most support agent deflection problems aren't model problems — they're documentation problems. Here's how to diagnose yours and build a feedback loop that compounds over time.
- [What is Agent Context Engineering? And How is it Different from Prompt and Harness Engineering?](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/agent-context-engineering): Learn what agent context engineering is, how it differs from prompt and harness engineering, why most agent failures are context failures, and a four-layer framework for building reliable AI agents.
- [Documentation Drift Is a Detection Problem, Not a Writing Problem](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/documentation-drift-detection-problem): Most teams treat documentation drift as a writing problem. It's not. The real bottleneck is detection — knowing what changed before the damage is done.
- [What It Looks Like When Your Docs Keep Up With Your Product](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/how-teams-keep-docs-up-to-date-with-promptless): Three real workflows from teams using Promptless that show what changes when your tooling handles detection, context gathering, and the first draft.
- [A Practical Guide on How to Optimize Your Docs for Agents](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/agent-docs): A practical guide for technical writers on adapting documentation for AI agent consumption, grounded in how agents actually access and use docs today.
- [Ambient Capture: The Missing Layer in Modern Documentation](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/ambient-capture): Ambient capture is the intelligent listening and recommendation layer for docs owners, continuously detecting docs-impacting changes so writers can focus on analysis and communication rather than surveillance.
- [Why we're building Promptless](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/why-were-building-promptless): AI won't replace human labor. But people that use AI well will replace those that don't. Promptless exists to make sure as many people as possible are in the first group.
- [Parrots are underrated pets and tools](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/parrots-are-underrated-pets-and-tools): You know dozens of useful heuristics but can't hold them all in your head while doing actual work. LLMs can run those obvious-in-hindsight checks in parallel in the background, acting as cognitive prosthetics that apply known wisdom continuously so you don't have to remember to.
- [Writing code was hard, actually](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/writing-code-was-hard-actually): The revisionist claim that writing code was never the hard part arrives conveniently at the moment AI makes it cheap to produce. But the market, the machine, and the engineers who built it all tell a different story.
- ["I Have No Mouth, and I Must Scream"](https://promptless.ai/blog/technical/i-must-scream): What happens when you give an AI agent a Slack channel to complain in.
- [Launch Week: December 2025](https://promptless.ai/blog/product-updates/launch-week-december-2025): Five major features we launched this December, from custom voice matching to automated screenshot updates.
- [Running a Lightweight Research Paper Club](https://promptless.ai/blog/life-at-promptless/running-a-lightweight-research-paper-club): How we run a weekly paper club at Promptless that's surprisingly useful, surprisingly easy, and has led to tangible product improvements.
- [How Vellum uses Promptless to draft over 50% of their doc updates](https://promptless.ai/blog/customer-stories/vellum): By democratizing documentation contributions, every team member now drives content—making docs a true team sport powered by Promptless.

sampleDiffs: - What to do: Just have fun. Write clearly and put the most important information first within each section, which still helps human readers. But no need to reorganize your docs around positional bias effects. Just try to make the pages have distinct concepts, keep it logically coherent internally - something that you already do 😊.
